title: The Resonance Index
hypothesis: If archive records are frequency fragments arranged spirally and each playback degrades the tone irreversibly, visitors will experience loss as sensory distortion rather than visual absence. Long-press locking creates a paradox: preserving silences the record. SessionStorage ensures the entire archive vanishes when the tab closes, embodying true ephemerality.
dataModel: Seeded frequency records with spiral positions, decay counters (0-12), lock states, and interference zones. Each record carries a base frequency (200-800Hz), a text fragment ID (date + catalog code), and degradation history. Seed determines variable count between 14-27 records and initial decay distribution.
recordCountStrategy: Seeded from session start timestamp: hash modulo 14 plus 14 base yields 14-27 records. Seed also determines which records start pre-degraded (20-40% begin damaged), simulating an archive already in mid-collapse.
interactionModel: Click plays tone via Web Audio API and increments decay counter, shifting frequency upward and reducing sustain. Long-press (pointerdown held >1200ms) toggles lock state; locked records cannot decay further but emit visual resonance waves to neighbors in SVG. Pointermove near locked records amplifies interference patterns, distorting nearby SVG paths. Each action rewrites sessionStorage and mutates SVG path data, DOM classes, and aria-labels.
stateModel: SessionStorage with per-record objects: {freq, decay, locked, accessCount, spiralAngle, textFragment}. Decay is irreversible within session. Lock state persists but silences the record. Closing the tab annihilates all state permanently. No recovery. Each reload generates a new archive from a different seed.
visualLanguage: Monochrome spiral on near-black background. Records as SVG circles with frequency-proportional radius. Decay shown as path vertex distortion and opacity reduction. Locked records pulse with slow SVG animate. Interference zones render as overlapping sine-wave SVG paths between locked neighbors. Text IDs (dates, catalog codes) fade proportionally to degradation. No color, no glow.
expectedStructuralChange: First audio-primary interaction model in the archive. Introduces spiral spatial layout replacing all previous grid and linear arrangements. Long-press replaces drag as primary gesture. SessionStorage replaces localStorage, making ephemerality literal. Degradation affects audio frequency and sustain, not just visual state. Locked records create emergent interference patterns with neighbors, introducing systemic consequences to individual actions.
